news_catalyst.headline: Global semiconductor selloff: SK Hynix -10%, Samsung -6.4%, ASML -3.8%; Broadcom guidance miss and weak jobs print driving
news_catalyst.summary: TSM is collateral damage in a global semiconductor selloff triggered by Broadcom's Q3 AI guidance miss and the weak 172K US jobs print. South Korea's Kospi fell 5.5% with SK Hynix -10% and Samsung -6.4%. ASML lost 3.8% in Europe. TSM as the world's dominant advanced chip foundry is selling off on the fear that hyperscaler AI capex guidance will be reduced following Broadcom's miss. Today's 1.34x above-average volume on a -2.27% day shows genuine institutional selling.

technical.summary: TSM has given back all of the June 29-30 recovery (from $432 to $477). The $429.50 intraday low approaches the June 26 range bottom ($432). A close below $432 would establish a lower low confirming distribution. Two consecutive elevated-volume down days (Jul 1 -6.98%, today -2.27%) in four days suggest supply is not exhausted. DCF distorted by TWD/USD conversion; qualitative bull case (irreplaceable foundry) remains intact long-term.

wyckoff_narrative: TSM is in a consolidation range $430-480. Two elevated-volume down days in the range's lower half suggest supply is winning short-term. Watch $429-432 as critical support; breakdown on volume opens $400-410.

valuation.summary: DCF model shows $20,185 (TWD/USD conversion distortion, not usable). Qualitatively TSMC commands a structural premium as the sole manufacturer of leading-node AI chips at scale.

risks: Close below $429-432 (range bottom) on elevated volume opens $400-410 test
TSMC earnings mid-July — AI order pace commentary will drive sharp move
